metadata.summary: Storybook 8 with CSF 3, addon-a11y, addon-interactions, and Chromatic visual regression. Stories co-located with components, test-runner runs headless Playwright in CI.

content.title: Storybook Setup
content.summary: Storybook 8 with CSF 3 (Meta<typeof Component>, StoryObj, tags: ['autodocs']) wired to @storybook/addon-a11y, @storybook/addon-interactions, and Chromatic for visual regression. Stories co-located with components. @storybook/test-runner runs headless Playwright over all stories in CI. Every story must render without console errors before merge.

why.p: Storybook gives agents and designers a sandboxed component catalog with an args matrix for every variant. Combined with Chromatic visual snapshots and addon-a11y, it catches regressions that unit tests miss. The test-runner oracle makes CI pass/fail deterministic — without it, broken stories only surface during manual review.

list.item: Initial Storybook 8.x install on Vite/Webpack/Next.js project.
Wiring .storybook/main.ts and .storybook/preview.ts for theming, viewports, backgrounds, a11y addon.
Adding visual, a11y, and interaction testing (Chromatic / test-runner / addon-a11y).
Setting up CSF 3 stories and tags: ['autodocs'] for a typed component library.
Project ships only one or two pages — Ladle boots faster with less config.
No design system or shared component library yet — Storybook's value lives in component reuse.
Team uses playground/-style Next.js dev pages and would duplicate that effort.

rule.statement: Use @storybook/react-vite framework adapter for Vite projects. Configure reactDocgen with a propFilter to exclude node_modules props from autodocs.
rule.rationale: Without propFilter, every inherited HTMLButtonAttributes prop appears in the controls panel, burying the useful component props.

example.title: main.ts with essentials and a11y

section.name: preview-ts-configuration

rule.statement: Import globals.css in preview.ts for Tailwind. Define theme globalType with toolbar. Apply padding decorator. Add background and viewport parameters for all stories.
rule.rationale: Missing globals.css import is the number-one cause of "Tailwind classes don't work in Storybook". The theme globalType without a consuming decorator has no effect.

section.reference: Full preview.ts with theme, backgrounds, viewports

rule.statement: Use satisfies Meta<typeof Component> for the meta object and StoryObj<typeof meta> for stories. Always add tags: ['autodocs'] to generate a docs page.
rule.rationale: satisfies provides stronger type inference than a direct type annotation; without tags: ['autodocs'] no documentation page appears in Storybook 8.

example.title: CSF 3 story with variants

rule.statement: Always await every userEvent call inside a play function. Use step() for multi-step interactions to produce readable test reports.
rule.rationale: userEvent is async; without await the DOM update has not happened when expect() runs, producing flaky assertion failures.

example.title: Play function with userEvent and assertions

section.name: test-runner-ci-setup

rule.statement: Run @storybook/test-runner after storybook build in CI. Build the static export first; do not run test-runner against the dev server.
rule.rationale: The dev server is not deterministic (HMR, timing); the static build is reproducible and correctly represents what Chromatic will snapshot.

section.reference: GitHub Actions workflow for build + test-runner

rule.statement: Run Chromatic on every PR. Treat baseline acceptance as a human step — auto-accept only on the main branch merge.
rule.rationale: Auto-accepting on PRs defeats the visual review gate; regressions accumulate silently until a release reveals them.

rule.statement: Replace argTypesRegex action pattern with @storybook/addon-actions decorators. Do not copy Storybook 6.x action config.
rule.rationale: argTypesRegex for auto-actions is deprecated in Storybook 8 and produces console warnings that pollute CI logs.

antipattern.description: Using @storybook/react framework adapter for Next.js App Router instead of @storybook/nextjs.
antipattern.reason: Without @storybook/nextjs, useRouter returns null in stories, causing every story that imports a link component to throw.

antipattern.description: Adding duplicate addons entries in main.ts from multi-pass agent edits.
antipattern.reason: Duplicate addon entries cause addon panels to be empty; Storybook starts but the controls/a11y/actions tabs are invisible.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: First action: run npx storybook@latest init in the target repo and let it auto-detect the framework. Then have a subagent fill main.ts, preview.ts, and a starter Button.stories.tsx from the README skeleton. Add @storybook/addon-a11y and Chromatic in a second pass — small commits avoid blowing up the initial setup. Use a Playwright + @storybook/test-runner smoke test as the oracle ("storybook builds and every story renders without console errors").

section.name: limitations
section.p: Storybook 8 dropped Webpack 4 and Babel-only setups; legacy Create React App projects need a Vite migration first. react-docgen-typescript chokes on generic components (<T>) and emits empty prop tables in autodocs — needs propFilter + sometimes a manual argTypes block. Tailwind v4 + Storybook 8 needs the @tailwindcss/postcss plugin loaded via viteFinal / webpackFinal; the init command does not configure it. Theme switching via globalTypes.toolbar only flips a CSS class — agents often forget to actually consume it in decorators and the toggle does nothing. Storybook 8 changed the argTypesRegex action behavior; agents copying older snippets get console warnings on startup.

section.name: best-practices
section.p: Pick CSF 3 (Meta<typeof Component>, StoryObj) for new stories; CSF 2 still works but autodocs and controls are weaker. Co-locate stories with components (Button.tsx + Button.stories.tsx) — ergonomics over global stories/ folder. Keep preview.ts small: globals (theme, locale), decorators, parameters. Avoid app-wide providers unless strictly needed. Use argTypes + parameters: { controls: { exclude: [...] } } to hide noise from autodocs; default tables are full of HTML attribute leaks. Run @storybook/test-runner in CI on every PR — catches "story renders but throws" regressions cheaply. Treat the deployed Storybook URL as a design-review artifact for non-engineers; commit messages should reference story IDs.

section.name: cli-tools-and-services
section.p: npx storybook@latest init: Bootstrap with framework auto-detect. npx storybook@latest upgrade: Major-version upgrade with codemods. npx storybook@latest doctor: Diagnose plugin/version mismatches. pnpm dlx @storybook/test-runner: Headless Playwright runner over all stories. npx chromatic --project-token=$T: Visual review + Storybook hosting. pnpm dlx storybook build: Static export for hosting (storybook-static/). Chromatic (SaaS): Default for visual testing; agents read PR-status checks and capture baselines. Storybook hosting on faion-net: storybook-static/ rsynced to storybook.faion.net. Vercel/Netlify: Drop storybook-static for previews. Percy: Alternative to Chromatic; same agent flow. Lost Pixel: Self-hosted visual regression; agents can drive via CLI.

template.purpose: Storybook main.ts with react-vite, essentials, interactions, a11y, Chromatic addon wired.
preview.ts with theme globalType, background/viewport parameters, padding decorator.
GitHub Actions workflow: build Storybook + run test-runner headless.
